description: Atlassian Cloud security for Jira Software, Jira Service Management, Confluence, and Atlassian Connect/Forge apps. Covers API tokens, OAuth 2.0 (3LO), Connect JWT, Server/DC personal access tokens, and space/project sharing misconfigurations.

token_patterns.name: Jira / Confluence Personal Access Token (Server / DC)
Atlassian Connect JWT (asymmetric)
token_patterns.notes: JWT pattern is broad; require an Atlassian-context hotword to avoid generic JWT false positives.

misconfigurations.title: Confluence / Jira space configured for anonymous access
misconfigurations.rationale: Public-internet-readable spaces leak roadmaps, vuln tickets, and PII repeatedly in the public record.
misconfigurations.fix: In each Confluence space: Space settings > Permissions > remove 'Anonymous' user. For Jira: Project settings > Permissions > remove 'Anyone' from 'Browse Projects'.

misconfigurations.title: Confluence global default permission allows public access
misconfigurations.rationale: Global default page access overrides space-level intentions if not flipped.
misconfigurations.fix: Atlassian admin > Confluence > Global permissions > set Anonymous Access to off.

misconfigurations.title: Single personal API token used by multiple integrations
misconfigurations.rationale: Atlassian Cloud API tokens are bound to a human account. Sharing across services means token leak compromises the human's full account.
misconfigurations.fix: Issue one token per integration via a dedicated bot user, or migrate to OAuth 2.0 (3LO) Connected Apps with scoped permissions.

misconfigurations.title: MFA not enforced for site-admin / org-admin accounts
misconfigurations.rationale: Atlassian site admins can install apps with elevated privilege; ATO is catastrophic.
misconfigurations.fix: admin.atlassian.com > Security > Two-step verification policy — enforce for all members of Site-admins and Organization Admins groups.

misconfigurations.title: Forge/Connect app installation allowed by any user
misconfigurations.rationale: Malicious or compromised Marketplace apps are a recurring SaaS supply-chain vector.
misconfigurations.fix: admin.atlassian.com > Apps > require admin approval for app installation.

misconfigurations.title: ScriptRunner / Behaviours scripts installed without code review
misconfigurations.rationale: These run with Jira admin privilege and can mutate any issue, project, or user.
misconfigurations.fix: Gate ScriptRunner changes through PR review; export current scripts via the REST API and version-control them.

admin_red_flags.title: Bulk Jira issue / Confluence page export by an unusual principal
admin_red_flags.rationale: Export-as-CSV / export-as-PDF for a whole project is a classic exfil step.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `Export issues` or `Export space` actions outside business hours / by non-owners.

admin_red_flags.title: Permission scheme modified to grant 'Browse' to 'Anyone'
admin_red_flags.rationale: ATO actors widen permissions to make later silent data theft easier.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `Permission scheme updated` with `grants` containing `anonymous` or `group=anyone`.

admin_red_flags.title: New Marketplace app installed
admin_red_flags.rationale: Track install events; correlate with abnormal API-call patterns within 24h.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `App installed` events; alert on installs by anyone outside the IT admin group.

admin_red_flags.title: Multiple API tokens created for the same user within a short window
admin_red_flags.rationale: Repeated token creation often follows credential phishing.
admin_red_flags.signal: id.atlassian.com audit: `api_token_created` count per account > 3 in 24h.
